findings.evidence: `Gradings.lean:108` has `theorem alexander_eq ... G.alexander x = (2 * (G.JX x - G.JO x) + ...)/2`, so the new line `119` theorem is obtained by `rw [G.alexander_eq x, G.alexander_eq y]; ring`.
findings.fix: Remove this theorem and prove the rectangle theorem by rewriting both sides with `G.alexander_eq x` and `G.alexander_eq y` followed by the existing `JX`/`JO` rectangle lemmas and `ring`.
findings.issue: `GridDiagram.alexander_sub_eq_JX_sub_JO` is parallel API for the existing `GridDiagram.alexander_eq` normal form rather than new rectangle material.

summary: The rectangle-specific material appears to reuse the existing point-set decomposition and `J` additivity, but the PR adds a general Alexander-difference API that is already a one-line consequence of the existing normal form.
